name: Web Theme Conformance Demo
summary: The runnable proof that the Web Theme token-resolution standard is conformant. The runtime (resolveTokens + compileToCss) is relocated to Frontier UI (#1907, WE holds zero executable); its conformance runs through the plateau-hosted conformance runner (#1788 (b)) driving the WE-owned webtheme vector suite (#1908: a DTCG document in, the resolved token map out) against the FUI binding. webtheme is non-verdict — the observable is the resolved token map, judged by deep-equal (#1816). The pass/fail panel is rendered at plateau-origin inside a cross-origin iframe.
description:
Concept

The runnable proof that the Web Theme token-resolution standard is conformant. The runtime — the resolveTokens DTCG walk and compileToCss projection — is relocated to Frontier UI (#1907; WE holds zero executable, #1282). Its conformance runs through the plateau-hosted conformance runner (#1788 ratified (b)), driving the WE webtheme vector suite (#1908) against the FUI binding.

Verified contract

webtheme is a non-verdict standard: the conformance subject is the resolved token map that resolveTokens projects a DTCG document onto, judged by the deep-equal matcher (#1816) — never the compiled CSS string. WE owns only the build-agnostic vectors and the @webeverything/contracts/webtheme schema; the engine impl is FUI's, the runner is plateau's. The pass/fail panel is rendered at plateau-origin inside an iframe — the cross-origin boundary (#1788 ratified (b)) reaching the shared, neutral runner with no implementer→runner edge. A green result proves conformance independent of how resolution is built (#899).
